exercises.example_sentence: [ファイル1]これはようが〜まいがの例文です。
exercises.translation: This is an example sentence for ようが〜まいが.
wrong_choice_explanations: であろうが is a variant of ようが, but uses the copula instead of the potential form.
にせよ marks 'whether / in either case', a general concessive marker.
ともあれ means 'in any case / at any rate', a conclusion marker rather than conditional.

exercises.example_sentence: [ファイル10]これはようが〜まいがの例文です。
exercises.translation: This is an example sentence for ようが〜まいが.
wrong_choice_explanations: 'にせよ' does not express the same grammatical relationship as '〜ようが〜まいが' in this context.
'でさえ' does not express the same grammatical relationship as '〜ようが〜まいが' in this context.
'たとしても' does not express the same grammatical relationship as '〜ようが〜まいが' in this context.

exercises.example_sentence: [ファイル2]これはようが〜まいがの例文です。
exercises.translation: This is an example sentence for ようが〜まいが.
wrong_choice_explanations: While であろうが exists as a pattern, this sentence requires ようがまいが to match the intended grammatical and semantic structure.
While にせよ exists as a pattern, this sentence requires ようがまいが to match the intended grammatical and semantic structure.
While ともあれ exists as a pattern, this sentence requires ようがまいが to match the intended grammatical and semantic structure.

exercises.example_sentence: [ファイル3]これはようが〜まいがの例文です。
exercises.translation: This is an example sentence for ようが〜まいが.
wrong_choice_explanations: Means 'whether is or not'; less structured than the parallel construction.
Means 'whether'; doesn't convey the do-or-not parallel structure.
Means 'in any case'; less balanced than the yes-or-no construction.

exercises.example_sentence: [ファイル4]これはようが〜まいがの例文です。
exercises.translation: This is an example sentence for ようが〜まいが.
wrong_choice_explanations: means "whether it is or is not"; uses different structure.
means "even if"; is less parallel than the ようが〜まいが construction.
means "at any rate"; does not express the parallelism of the original pattern.

exercises.example_sentence: [ファイル5]これはようが〜まいがの例文です。
exercises.translation: This is an example sentence for ようが〜まいが.
wrong_choice_explanations: であろうが means 'whether it is or not'; it doesn't convey the 'whether one does or not' meaning needed here.
にせよ means 'even if / whether or not'; it doesn't convey the 'whether one does or not' meaning needed here.
ともあれ means 'in any case / at any rate'; it doesn't convey the 'whether one does or not' meaning needed here.

exercises.example_sentence: [ファイル6]これはようが〜まいがの例文です。
exercises.translation: This is an example sentence for ようが〜まいが.
wrong_choice_explanations: にせよ is a conditional alternation but doesn't express the indifference to outcome that ようが~まいが conveys.
でさえ means 'even' and emphasizes something surprising, not the balanced 'whether or not' of ようが~まいが.
たとしても means 'even if' but expresses a condition with implication, not the neutral alternative of ようが~まいが.

exercises.translation: This is an example sentence for ようが〜まいが.
